value.user_objective: Make brandbuild.online work again after a production application error and preserve the correct state durably.

key_findings: The production error was caused by the domain serving an older deployment, not by a newly introduced code regression in the local workspace.
Memory had diverged across docs/project-memory, .project-memory, and project-memory and needed deliberate consolidation.
The repo builds cleanly, but Next.js warns that multiple lockfiles cause workspace-root inference.

value.user_objective: Fix the live BrandBuild server-side crash showing the global error screen and make the site work again immediately.
actions_taken: Confirmed the live domain and current production deployment were reachable.
Reproduced that public routes were healthy in a real browser and narrowed the likely failure to authenticated dashboard initialization.
Hardened the studio persistence client so server-side auth or Supabase initialization failures return null instead of throwing through the dashboard path.
Re-ran typecheck and build successfully.
Deployed the fix to Vercel production and verified brandbuild.online, /login, and the signed-out /dashboard redirect in a browser.
key_findings: The live domain was on a ready deployment, so the incident was not caused by a broken alias.
Public routes were healthy, which pointed to a user-session or dashboard initialization failure path.
The dashboard read client could throw before the repository fallback logic ran, making an authenticated request vulnerable to the global error boundary.
memory_updates: Recorded the production incident, the client hardening change, and the remaining Dynamic server usage build warning.
unresolved_items: The marketing route still logs a non-fatal Dynamic server usage warning during build.
Supabase Auth dashboard setup remains incomplete.
Live provider runs still need full validation.
next_recommended_actions: Clean up the root-route cookie access so the build log is quieter and more trustworthy.
Continue with Supabase Auth setup and provider validation.

value.user_objective: Fix the live BrandBuild error page that was still appearing during login and auth-link handling.
actions_taken: Loaded BrandBuild project memory and treated the issue as a production incident.
Inspected the dashboard, auth, Supabase server, and middleware boundaries instead of assuming the latest UI batch was the direct cause.
Pulled Vercel deploy output and runtime logging commands to narrow the failure toward the auth/session boundary.
Hardened middleware session refresh so bad cookies or Supabase session-refresh failures return a safe anonymous response instead of throwing.
Hardened auth email-action handling so callback failures redirect back to login with a safe error instead of hitting the global error page.
Re-ran typecheck and production build successfully.
Deployed the fix to Vercel production and re-aliased brandbuild.online.
Verified the live root returns 200 after the incident deployment.
key_findings: The newly shipped assets workspace was build-clean and not the most likely root cause of the live error page.
The remaining brittle paths were middleware session refresh and auth callback handling around bad cookies, expired links, or transient Supabase failures.
memory_updates: Recorded the auth/session hardening as a production reliability fix and preserved the exact deployment URL.
unresolved_items: Supabase Auth dashboard setup remains incomplete.
Live provider runs still need full validation.
Campaign-level sequencing across selected outputs is still unbuilt.
next_recommended_actions: Push the auth-boundary incident fix to origin/main.
Keep watching the login and callback paths until the user confirms the global error page is gone.
Continue with Supabase Auth dashboard setup and real provider validation.

value.user_objective: Fix the public-site login button so it actually takes users to the login page.
actions_taken: Loaded BrandBuild project memory and inspected the public header, homepage, marketing media surfaces, login route, and redirect helpers.
Confirmed the actual /login route was healthy and narrowed the UX issue to the public marketing shell rather than the auth page itself.
Updated the public header and footer so access CTAs point directly to /login rather than relying on a /dashboard redirect.
Marked decorative marketing overlays, video frames, and preview layers as pointer-events-none so they cannot intercept clicks or taps.
Re-ran typecheck and production build successfully.
Deployed production to Vercel and re-aliased brandbuild.online.
Verified the live root returned 200 after the deployment.
Committed the batch as 74eafe4 and pushed main so git matches the live fix.
key_findings: The public-site login path issue was a UX and hit-testing problem, not a missing /login route.
On smaller screens, the visible header CTA was effectively the primary access path, so sending it to /dashboard created unnecessary dependence on protected-route redirect behavior.
Decorative marketing overlays should be explicitly non-interactive so they cannot interfere with CTA clicks.
memory_updates: Recorded the public login-path hardening and the new navigation/hit-testing rule for future marketing work.
unresolved_items: Supabase Auth dashboard setup remains incomplete.
Live provider runs still need full validation.
Campaign-level sequencing across selected outputs is still unbuilt.
next_recommended_actions: Verify the live /login path in a browser session once the user tests the updated site.
Continue with Supabase Auth dashboard setup and real provider validation.

value.user_objective: Continue autonomously on the highest-value live integration blockers and make BrandBuild more usable end to end.
actions_taken: Reloaded project memory and confirmed the active blockers.
Inspected the raw OpenAI video status payload for the completed Sora job.
Verified that completed Sora media is retrieved from /v1/videos/{video_id}/content.
Added a BrandBuild proxy route for completed generation media.
Updated the Sora adapter and generation output mapping to use the content endpoint truthfully.
Refactored shot uploads and storage-backed asset URL resolution to use authenticated Supabase storage access.
Added a Supabase storage migration for the assets bucket and authenticated storage policies.
Updated the provider smoke harness to reflect the new upload/mirroring readiness split.
Ran typecheck, production build, a live Sora smoke-status check, and deployed production.
key_findings: The raw OpenAI video status object for a completed Sora render does not include a direct media URL.
The real media is available from the authenticated /v1/videos/{video_id}/content endpoint.
Shot uploads do not inherently require SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; they can run through authenticated Supabase storage when the bucket and policies exist.
Server-side storage credentials are still needed for backend-only provider-output mirroring.
memory_updates: Updated current state, tasks, file index, and knowledge to reflect the Sora content proxy and storage-auth upload architecture.
Mirrored canonical structured memory into .project-memory.
Updated root project-memory operating files with the new deployment and next steps.
unresolved_items: Supabase storage migration still needs to be applied to the live project.

Kling account still needs balance.
Higgsfield credentials are still invalid.

Validate a real signed-in upload and Sora dashboard generation.
Finish Supabase Auth dashboard configuration.
Unblock Kling and Higgsfield for live dashboard validation.

user_objective: Make BrandBuild actually work when creating campaigns, verify uploads and generation together, and build any control tooling needed to validate the integrations.
actions_taken: Inspected the create-campaign flow, the shot upload route, generation route, provider adapters, environment state, and current project memory before editing.
Repaired campaign creation by moving it onto POST /api/campaigns and changing the modal to client-side fetch with inline success and error handling.
Confirmed that the live deployment and local app env do not currently have SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, which means the private upload path cannot succeed yet.
Updated the shot uploader and shot detail page so uploads show a clear blocked state when storage is not configured instead of pretending the feature is ready.
Updated the control-center system audit so compare outputs and handoff are treated as shipped while private uploads are correctly shown as blocked without server-side storage credentials.
Added a reusable provider smoke-test harness at apps/dashboard/scripts/provider-smoke.mjs and wired it into package.json.
Ran the live smoke test against the current provider credentials.
Verified the repo still passes typecheck and build.
Deployed the latest batch to Vercel production and verified brandbuild.online returned 200.
Pushed origin/main through commit 1a8b5b3 so GitHub matches the latest deployed state.
key_findings: Campaign creation was still brittle at the server-action boundary and needed a dedicated API path with inline UI error handling.
The live deployment is missing SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, so private uploads cannot work yet even though the upload route and UI exist.

A live provider smoke test produced concrete integration results: Sora accepted a queued job, Kling failed because the account balance is not enough, and Higgsfield failed because the current credentials are invalid.
memory_updates: Recorded the campaign-creation repair, storage-readiness blocker, provider smoke-test outcomes, and the new reusable smoke-test script.
unresolved_items: Production still needs SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY before private uploads can work.
Kling still needs account funding before live jobs can proceed.
Higgsfield still needs valid credentials before live jobs can proceed.
next_recommended_actions: Add SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to the Vercel project and local app env, then test a real private upload from the shot page.
Poll the queued Sora job to capture the full live success or failure path.
Fund Kling and rerun the smoke test plus an in-product generation.
Fix or rotate Higgsfield credentials and rerun the smoke test plus an in-product generation.

user_objective: Unblock the shot workflow and continue full auto while Supabase storage rollout remains an external dependency.

Added a curated BrandBuild reference-sample registry plus built-in sample preview assets.

Extended asset persistence to create external_link records for linked and imported references.
Rebuilt the shot asset uploader UI so private upload, hosted reference linking, and sample import all live in one premium workspace.
Re-ran typecheck and production build successfully.
key_findings: The current app no longer needs to wait on storage rollout for every reference workflow.
A hosted HTTPS link path plus built-in sample library can unblock real operator testing without pretending private uploads are already live.
The existing shot asset route was flexible enough to absorb the new modes without route churn.
memory_updates: Recorded the no-storage reference fallback architecture and promoted it into durable product memory.
Updated current blockers, next steps, tasks, file index, and integration status to reflect that private upload rollout is no longer the only way forward.
unresolved_items: The Supabase assets-bucket migration still needs to be applied before authenticated private uploads work end to end on production.
Kling still needs account funding.
Higgsfield still needs valid credentials.
Supabase branded auth/email setup is still manual.
next_recommended_actions: Deploy the hosted-reference and sample-import batch to production.
Validate a signed-in Sora dashboard run using the new fallback reference path.
Apply the Supabase storage migration and then validate authenticated private uploads.
